claim: Claude Code loads skills from four scopes with priority enterprise > personal > project, and plugins get their own namespace; personal is ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md and project is .claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md.
quote:
| Personal   | `~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`            | All your projects              |
| Project    | `.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`              | This project only              |
| Plugin     | `<plugin>/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`             | Where plugin is enabled        |

claim: A skill is a directory whose SKILL.md carries YAML frontmatter (name, description, disable-model-invocation, allowed-tools, etc.) plus markdown instructions, and may include supporting files (templates, examples, scripts).
quote: Every skill needs a `SKILL.md` file with two parts: YAML frontmatter (between `---` markers) that tells Claude when to use the skill, and markdown content with instructions Claude follows when the skill is invoked.

claim: Claude Code watches skill directories live: edits under ~/.claude/skills, project .claude/skills, and --add-dir .claude/skills apply in-session without restart, but a brand-new top-level skills dir requires restart.
quote: Claude Code watches skill directories for file changes. Adding, editing, or removing a skill under `~/.claude/skills/`, the project `.claude/skills/`, or a `.claude/skills/` inside an `--add-dir` directory takes effect within the current session without restarting.

claim: Skills can pre-approve tools via the allowed-tools frontmatter so Claude runs them without per-use prompts, independent of --dangerously-skip-permissions, and baseline permission settings still govern unlisted tools.
quote: The `allowed-tools` field grants permission for the listed tools while the skill is active, so Claude can use them without prompting you for approval. It does not restrict which tools are available: every tool remains callable, and your [permission settings](/en/permissions) still govern tools that are not listed.

claim: Anthropic's reference devcontainer is the sanctioned place to run claude --dangerously-skip-permissions; the container's firewall and isolation are what make YOLO mode defensible.
quote: The container's enhanced security measures (isolation and firewall rules) allow you to run `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` to bypass permission prompts for unattended operation.

claim: Anthropic's first-party anthropics/skills repo ships document skills (docx, pdf, pptx, xlsx) plus example skills, and is installed via /plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills followed by /plugin install document-skills@anthropic-agent-skills.
quote:
/plugin marketplace add anthropics/skills

# Then install specific skill sets:
/plugin install document-skills@anthropic-agent-skills
/plugin install example-skills@anthropic-agent-skills

claim: The document skills in anthropics/skills are source-available (not OSS) while the rest of the repo is Apache 2.0.
quote: **License**: These document skills are **source-available** (not open source), provided as reference implementations for production AI applications.

claim: obra/superpowers is distributed as a Claude Code plugin via /plugin install superpowers@claude-plugins-official (official marketplace) or /plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace, and is multi-platform (Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Copilot CLI, OpenCode).
quote:
**Claude Code (Official):** Install via Anthropic's marketplace with `/plugin install superpowers@claude-plugins-official`

**Claude Code (Superpowers Marketplace):** Register the marketplace first (`/plugin marketplace add obra/superpowers-marketplace`)

claim: The common community preload pattern is a plain git clone into ~/.claude/skills, e.g. git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers ~/.claude/skills/superpowers, and Claude Code auto-detects new skill folders.
quote: To install obra's superpowers collection (20+ battle-tested skills), use: `git clone https://github.com/obra/superpowers ~/.claude/skills/superpowers`

claim: Docker itself ships a docker model skills --claude command that writes skill definitions into ~/.claude/skills (or a custom --dest), with --force to overwrite — useful as a Dockerfile RUN step.
quote:
Docker places Claude Code skills in `~/.claude/skills` when you use the `--claude` flag.

**Command Invocation:**
To install skills for Claude Code, you run:
```
docker model skills --claude
```

claim: Anthropic's official line is that Skills teach behavior (procedural knowledge, cheap tokens, on-demand) while MCP servers supply connectivity/tools (heavier, often tens of thousands of tokens); the two compose.
quote: Skills = procedural knowledge (30-50 tokens each, loaded on-demand) MCP = external tool connections (can use 50k+ tokens)

claim: The official anthropics/claude-plugins-official marketplace is auto-registered at startup, and /plugin, /plugin install <plugin>@<marketplace>, /reload-plugins are the interactive install path; plugins bundle skills, commands, subagents, MCP servers, and hooks together.
quote: The official Anthropic marketplace (claude-plugins-official) is automatically available when you start Claude Code... Plugins are a lightweight way to package and share any combination of: Slash commands, Subagents, MCP servers, and Hooks.
